name: Loch Ness, Glencoe & Scottish Highlands Day Trip from Edinburgh
tagline: Eilean Donan Castle, the mysterious Loch Ness, and the wild Glencoe valley in one epic day
overview: One of Scotland's most celebrated day tours takes you deep into the Highlands, past the brooding Glencoe valley, along the legendary Loch Ness, and to the impossibly photogenic Eilean Donan Castle — arguably the most photographed castle in Scotland. Small group size ensures a personal experience.
highlights: Visit Eilean Donan Castle, Scotland's most iconic fortress
Cruise on Loch Ness with monster-spotting opportunity
Drive through the dramatic Glencoe valley
Small-group tour (max 16) for a personal experience
Expert Scottish guide with local knowledge

itinerary.description: Visit the most photographed castle in Scotland, sitting on a small island where three lochs meet. Tour the medieval banqueting hall, armoury, and the Jacobite memorial room.
Optional boat cruise on the world-famous loch. Visit the Loch Ness Centre and see the ruins of Urquhart Castle on the shores.
Scenic stop in the dramatic Highland valley made famous by the 1692 massacre and featured in James Bond and Harry Potter films.

meeting_point: Edinburgh city centre — confirmed at booking
tips: Departs very early (around 6:30–7am) — get a good night's sleep
Dress in warm layers; Highland weather is unpredictable year-round
The small group size (max 16) makes this feel like a private tour

faqs.question: Is Eilean Donan Castle entry included?
faqs.answer: Transport to the castle is included; the entry fee (approx £8) is paid on arrival. Check your specific booking for updates.

faqs.question: Can I see the Loch Ness monster?
faqs.answer: The monster remains unconfirmed, but the cruise gives you the best possible chance. The loch's scenery alone is worth the trip.

hero_image.alt: Eilean Donan Castle reflected in the still waters of Loch Duich at dawn

meta.title: Loch Ness & Eilean Donan Castle Day Trip from Edinburgh
meta.description: Explore Eilean Donan Castle, Loch Ness and Glencoe on a small-group day trip from Edinburgh. From £45 per person. 18,000+ reviews.
